reasoning: The 5-year constant maturity yield closed at 4.09 on March 14, a local spike up from recent lows near 3.97. The statistical trend analysis from FRED indicates strong downward momentum over the past month, yielding a linear model probability of only 0.26 that the yield will exceed 4.09 by March 23. Factoring in historical seasonality brings the short-term algorithmic estimate to 0.46. Recent news and expert analyses (e.g., March Reuters poll) highlight escalating recession concerns outstripping inflation risks, keeping market sentiment tilted toward further (albeit gradual) rate cuts in 2025, suggesting yields have slightly more downside than upside moving forward. I am forecasting initial probabilities below 0.5 because expected mean reversion and fundamental outlooks point lower, but monotonically grading my probabilities dynamically closer to 0.50 for distant resolution dates to accurately reflect compounding macroeconomic uncertainty over extended horizons.
